capabilities: Combine dependency-driven action sequencing with AI prompting to enhance agent planning efficiency and accuracy.

procedure.action: Extract dependencies using a directed acyclic graph (DAG) to model task sequences using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Compose AI prompting by training a model on domain-specific datasets to generate action sequences by composing module interfaces via adapter pattern; assert input/output type contracts match before connecting; assert integration_error_count < 1 after contract validation.

Invoke a constraint solver to enforce domain-specific rules on generated sequences by invoking the target API with the specified parameters; assert response_status < 400 and response_time_ms < 5000; log request_id and response_code.

Simulate sequences in a controlled environment to evaluate feasibility and correctness using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Log sequence execution metrics and adjust DAG or prompts based on performance data using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

summary: Specialize dependency-driven action sequencing for agent planning by integrating AI prompting techniques, ensuring domain-specific constraints are respected.
title: Dependency-Driven Action Sequencing in Agent Planning

failureConditions: Action sequences violate domain constraints; this occurs when the operation proceeds without pre-condition validation, allowing malformed input to propagate and corrupt downstream state silently.

Generated sequences are inefficient or non-optimal; this occurs when the operation proceeds without pre-condition validation, allowing malformed input to propagate and corrupt downstream state silently.
Dependency graph becomes cyclic, causing execution deadlocks.
metrics: input/output type contracts match before connecting
successConditions: Run unit tests to ensure sequences respect all domain constraints; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
Benchmark sequence execution time against baseline metrics; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
Validate that all dependencies are resolved before action execution; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
